description_long: This is a spiny, reddish-brown crustacean with a robust, oval-shaped body covered in short bristles and spines. Its carapace is rough and textured, with a slightly domed profile and a mottled coloration that ranges from deep red to lighter orange and cream hues. The creature has ten long, jointed legs, including two prominent front claws, all of which are covered in fine spines and hairs. Its eyes are set on short stalks at the front of the carapace, flanked by two visible antennae. The underside is lighter in color and less spiny, with the segmentation of the cephalothorax and abdomen faintly visible. The overall appearance is that of a marine crab, likely adapted for camouflage among rocks or seaweed.
description: A spiny, reddish-brown crab with a rough, bristled carapace and long, jointed legs, including two prominent claws. Its body is oval-shaped, with eyes on short stalks and visible antennae.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crab's face, both claws extended, and eyes on short stalks; the spiny texture and bristles are prominent.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the crab's profile, the curvature of the carapace, and the segmentation of the legs and body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broad, spiny back of the carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Right-side view similar to the left, showing the crab's profile, spiny legs, and the lighter underside.
